---
title: "![](images/icons/icon_11.png){.title-icon} Comorbidity"
subtitle: "Multimorbidity and a comorbidity score as a covariate"
date: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-warning}
**Under development.** Structural outline - to be expanded with concrete examples.
:::

Comorbidity at baseline is a common covariate. It is derived from diagnoses (LPR) up to the index date and summarised in a **score per person**. Use a **ready-made algorithm** rather than coding it from scratch.

A natural choice on DST is **NMI (the Nordic Multimorbidity Index)** - a weighted multimorbidity index developed and validated on Nordic register data. See [NMI](14c_nmi.qmd) for the concrete implementation, and [Algorithms & special packages](14a_pakker-oversigt.qmd) for the packages available on DST.

The pattern is the same whichever measure you choose: take your LPR diagnoses (filtered to the cohort and to the time **before index**), run them through the algorithm, and get a score per `pnr`, which you save as `.rds` and link in [Phase 12](11_data-manipulation.qmd).

```r
cohort_pnrs <- unique(readRDS("datasets/full_cohort.rds")$pnr)

diagnoses_before_index <- open_dataset("path/to/lpr_diag/") %>%
  rename_with(tolower) %>%
  filter(pnr %in% !!cohort_pnrs) %>%      # ONLY your cohort
  # ... get the contact date, keep diagnoses before index_date ...
  collect()
# → pass the diagnoses to the NMI algorithm to get a score per pnr
```

## See also
- [NMI](14c_nmi.qmd) · [Algorithms & special packages](14a_pakker-oversigt.qmd)
- [Phase 12 - Assemble and prepare the dataset](11_data-manipulation.qmd)
